vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on Backup gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow-, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow-

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only changes file permissions on a backup gshadow file, which is a standard STIG hardening step and does not affect active authentication services or system boot. The risk of lockout or service disruption is minimal.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Improper permissions could expose group password hashes; Incorrect chmod syntax could leave file overly permissive
